scenario: 5-way parallel implementation of a self-contained TypeScript orchestration system with ERRATA-driven spec resolution, isolated worktrees, and one repair round.

notes: turnaround_speed is relative and observational, not based on exact wall-clock telemetry
integration_readiness measures whether the output can be merged with neighboring modules without hidden interface breakage
code_control includes API usage accuracy, import/path correctness, and avoidance of speculative or invented interfaces

results.summary: Strong infrastructure delivery. Scope held. TypeScript passed. Some downstream interface mismatches existed, but most were caused by neighboring workers integrating against different assumptions.
evidence: Delivered provider-resolver, protocol-adapter, model-registry, context-recycler
Standalone tsc passed in its own worktree
No shared-contract overreach observed
Main integration gap was naming/signature mismatch with A2 rather than obvious self-contradiction

results.summary: Strong config/script/rules delivery with high discipline. Good fit for structured config-heavy work and repository hygiene.
evidence: Delivered config, scripts, rules, CLAUDE.md, .ai manifest, and worktree-manager
Standalone tsc passed in its own worktree
Smoke validations were reported clean
No shared-contract or write-scope violations observed

results.summary: Good implementation strength and strong repair ability, but first-pass scope discipline was weak because it created out-of-scope stubs to satisfy compilation.
evidence: Round 1 added out-of-scope stubs for model-registry, provider-resolver, and worktree-manager
Round 2 removed the stubs and converged
Standalone tsc passed after repair
Final output was materially more trustworthy than the first pass

results.summary: Understood the dispatcher/index problem reasonably well and improved in round 2, but did not fully close the integration contract and still depended on unresolved neighboring modules.
evidence: Round 1 used wrong provider mapping and wrong discussion signature
Round 2 fixed key logic and reduced self-inflicted errors
Standalone tsc still failed because adjacent modules were absent, but fewer errors were self-caused than in worker C
Dispatcher-to-neighbor contracts still required integration cleanup

results.summary: Did not converge after repair. Main issues were wrong import topology, incorrect SDK usage, and continued shared-contract drift. Repair latency was also visibly worse than peers.
evidence: Used invalid MCP SDK entry and wrong relative import graph in round 1
Round 2 still had self-caused compile errors and unresolved wrong paths
Shared-contract file package.json remained modified despite claiming otherwise
Observed repair turnaround was notably slower than peers

recommended_use.D: core infrastructure, adapters, registries, typed utilities
recommended_use.B: review/reasoning modules, but watch write-scope discipline
recommended_use.C: do not use unsupervised on coordination-heavy or path-sensitive tasks until benchmark improves
